response.justification: The candidate is a highly experienced generalist with strong testing culture, mentoring activity, and some impressive low-latency distributed systems work, but the profile misses the role's hard requirements. C++ exposure amounts to well under a year (one 7-month stint at National Instruments plus a thesis project), far short of the required 8 years, and C++ is absent from the skills list — the systems work is predominantly in DLang, TypeScript, and Node.js. Cloud experience is limited to deploying to Kubernetes and some AWS CDK product work rather than building or leading a compute platform, and there is no formal technical-leadership track record mentoring cloud or DevOps engineers.
strengths: Deep low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (microservices handling millions of requests/second at Sociomantic)
Strong engineering-quality culture: TDD, CI/CD, authored test frameworks, ran courses and refactoring dojos
Broad 25+ year career across web, embedded, and infrastructure with sustained open-source platform ownership
concerns: C++ experience (~7 months professional) falls drastically short of the 8-year minimum; primary systems language is DLang
No production cloud compute platform or large-scale cloud architecture ownership — cloud exposure is limited to app-level AWS/Kubernetes use
No formal technical-leadership role leading or mentoring cloud/DevOps engineering teams
